title: Eriostemon alpinus
taxonomy: Eriostemon alpinus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to eastern Australia and is commonly known as the Alpine Waxflower. The plant belongs to the genus Eriostemon, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. Within the Rutaceae family, Eriostemon alpinus is closely related to other species such as Boronia and Philotheca. The exact classification of Eriostemon alpinus can be further broken down into more specific ranks within the plant kingdom.

intro: Eriostemon alpinus, also known as the native rosemary or alpine rosemary, is a small, flowering plant native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating, thanks to its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Eriostemon alpinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
